---
title: Microsoft Purview — Audit & Retention State (Reserved Slot) — Adapter Technical
  Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: purview-audit
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: telemetry
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# Microsoft Purview — Audit & Retention State (Reserved Slot) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `telemetry` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Microsoft Purview — Audit & Retention State Adapter is the **read-only telemetry counterpart** to the change-making Purview surface that the [`m365`](../m365/m365.html) modernization adapter authors. As a conformance-class telemetry adapter, it continuously observes the data-governance configuration that determines whether an agency can produce evidence on demand for audit, DLP, retention, and information-protection controls.

The driving concern is **FINDING-008**, the documented FedRAMP GCC-Moderate Purview audit 180-day cliff. Standard Microsoft 365 audit retention is 180 days; Premium licensing extends to one year; the 10-year add-on extends further. An agency on Standard licensing has a hard, recurring cliff: events older than 180 days are unrecoverable for assessor review. This adapter activates FINDING-008 as a **live drift signal in monthly ConMon packs** (UIAO_132 / ADR-047) rather than a static one-shot finding, so the cliff is visible every assessment cycle until the agency upgrades licensing or accepts the residual risk.

The adapter observes seven Purview surfaces in parallel:

1. **Unified audit retention tier** — Standard (180d), Premium (1y), or 10-year add-on. Drives FINDING-008.
2. **Audit-event coverage** — whether the audited event categories include the workloads the substrate's other adapters report against.
3. **DLP policy state** — Data Loss Prevention rule presence and scope; sources control evidence for SC-4.
4. **Sensitivity-label policy state** — label taxonomy, scope, and auto-labelling policy; sources control evidence for MP-3.
5. **Information Protection coverage** — which workloads are covered by sensitivity labels and which are gaps.
6. **Litigation-hold state** — hold scope, duration, and the principals to which holds apply.
7. **Insider Risk policy state** — Insider Risk Management policies, scope, and detection signals.

Together these close three `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` registry-layer gaps for Purview-anchored controls (SC-4, MP-3, RA-2) — by observing them as live state rather than asserting them as static SSP narrative.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-058 / ADR-059. Activation will follow the standard sync-canon allocation flow; the read-only nature of the surface keeps activation low-risk.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `unified-audit-retention-tier`, `audit-event-coverage`, `dlp-policy-state`, `sensitivity-label-policy-state`, `information-protection-coverage`, `litigation-hold-state`, `insider-risk-policy-state`

**Reads:** Microsoft Purview / Compliance Center configuration via Microsoft Graph (where exposed) and the Security & Compliance PowerShell module (for surfaces not yet on Graph). Read-only client-credential OAuth and certificate-anchored Security & Compliance PowerShell sessions per the documented least-privilege role set.

**Emits:** `purview-audit-tier-findings.json` (current audit retention tier plus the FINDING-008 status), `purview-dlp-policy-state.json` (DLP rule inventory and scope), `purview-label-coverage.json` (sensitivity-label coverage map per workload), plus `EvidenceObject` records that map to the SC-4, MP-3, RA-2, AU-2, AU-11 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** modify any Purview configuration (audit tier, DLP rules, label policies, holds, Insider Risk policies) — those are bundled inside [`m365`](../m365/m365.html). Does not access protected content (audit log entries themselves, DLP-flagged content, held-mailbox messages) — observes policy state and coverage, not the protected payloads. Does not bypass licensing limits — when the tenant is on Standard licensing, the adapter reports the 180-day cliff rather than silently working around it.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `AU-2`, `AU-11`, `SC-4`, `MP-3`, `RA-2`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Audit-event coverage observation verifies which event categories are enabled and which workloads are in scope, producing the canonical evidence of what is being audited. |
| **AU-11** Audit Record Retention | Primary | Audit retention tier observation activates FINDING-008 as a live signal — Standard (180d) vs Premium (1y) vs 10-year add-on. |
| **SC-4** Information in Shared System Resources | Primary | DLP policy state inventory sources the control evidence for protections against cross-resource leakage. |
| **MP-3** Media Marking | Primary | Sensitivity-label policy state and Information Protection coverage source the control evidence for labelling and marking. |
| **RA-2** Security Categorization | Supporting | Information Protection coverage indicates how the agency has categorized information across workloads; gaps in coverage are categorization-completeness findings. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `interval` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Reserved slot per ADR-058 / ADR-059. Read-only telemetry counterpart to the change-making Purview surface bundled inside the [`m365`](../m365/m365.html) modernization adapter (modernization-registry, scope: `purview`).

Activates FINDING-008 ([fedramp-gcc-moderate-purview-audit-180day-cliff](../../../findings/fedramp-gcc-moderate-purview-audit-180day-cliff.html)) as a **live drift signal** in monthly ConMon packs (UIAO_132 / ADR-047), not a static finding. Sources control evidence for SC-4 (DLP), MP-3 (sensitivity labels), and RA-2 (information classification) — closes three `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` registry-layer gaps for Purview-anchored controls.

ZTMM pillar: data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-047 — Continuous Monitoring Pack lifecycle
- ADR-058 — Purview audit telemetry reserved slot
- ADR-059 — Purview audit telemetry surface scope
- UIAO_132 — Continuous Monitoring Pack
- FINDING-008 — FedRAMP GCC-Moderate Purview audit 180-day cliff

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
